description: Colourless liquid, essential for life. Freezes at 0 °C, boils at 100 °C.
Colourless gas. Turns limewater milky. Sublimes at −78 °C (dry ice).
Colourless, odourless gas. Highly flammable. Lightest element.
Colourless gas with a sharp smell. Dissolves in water to form hydrochloric acid.
Colourless gas with pungent odour. Turns damp red litmus blue. Used in fertilisers.
White solid. Strong alkali, highly soluble in water. Used in soap making.
White crystalline solid. Common table salt. Soluble in water.
White solid. Soluble in water. Used as a salt substitute.
White solid. Absorbs moisture. Used in de‑icing roads.
White powder. High melting point. Used as a refractory material.
White solid. Reacts vigorously with water (slaked lime). Used in cement.
Red‑brown solid. Main ore of iron. Used as pigment.
Colourless gas with a choking smell. Turns acidified potassium dichromate green.
Brown gas. Toxic. Responsible for acid rain.
Colourless, odourless gas. Highly toxic. Incomplete combustion product.
